crm_import.write_instruction: Use warden-crm counterparty_upsert, outreach_emit, and next_touch_set only after operator approval.

non_promotion_statement: Pass 0060 drafts outreach packets and CRM import shapes. It does not send outreach, write CRM records, prove buyer demand, prove budget, or promote any natural law.

acceptance_criteria: Buyer names one workflow where a proof packet would be reviewed by a real stakeholder.
Buyer identifies a source, action, verification, or replay gap in the current workflow.
Buyer names a concrete artifact that would move the conversation from interest to pilot.
Pilot candidate can be bounded to a falsifiable research claim or reproduction task.

counterparty_seed.name: Research Lab Proof-Packet Prospect
counterparty_seed.notes: Pass 0060 generated seed row; prospect identity is a placeholder until buyer discovery names a real organization.

evidence_intake_fields.label: Named buyer role and authority path
Budget owner, procurement path, or explicit no-budget signal
Current workflow pain stated by the buyer
Existing tools, platforms, observability, notebooks, or lab systems
What evidence current tools fail to bind
Concrete demo condition that would justify a pilot
One result that should stop the pilot
Formalization, replay, or experiment reproducibility requirement

follow_up_schedule.reason: Send draft proof-packet pilot note and request a 25-minute evidence-fit call.
Ask for one current workflow artifact or incumbent-tool screenshot equivalent.
Close loop with a go/no-go proof-demo acceptance criterion.

market_data_targets.target: Identify the buyer who can fund proof-packet pilots.
Measure what evidence threshold converts interest into a pilot.
Map current AI4Science workflow bottlenecks against agentic discovery platforms.
Collect proof or formalization requirements for math and scientific claims.
Collect top whitepapers, open problems, and reproducibility requirements per field.

negative_disqualifiers: Buyer only wants a generic chatbot, dashboard, or prose report.
Buyer cannot name any evidence artifact that current tools fail to provide.
Buyer expects unsupported claims of scientific discovery, market dominance, or production certification.
Research workflow cannot be bounded to replay, formalization, or reproducibility checks.
outreach_packets.subject: Proof-packet pilot for replayable AI4Science claims
outreach_packets.template_body:
We are testing whether research teams need claim-to-proof packets that bind sources, model actions, verification verdicts, and reproducibility evidence.

Pilot ask: Run one bounded pilot around a math, physics, biology, or AI4Science workflow where the acceptance criterion is replayable evidence rather than a claim of discovery.

Demo anchor: Pipeline-math++ style formal research proof packet.

Discovery question: When evaluating a Project Telos proof-packet workflow, how would your team answer this objection: We already have agentic discovery platforms; why is this different?

Boundary: this is a hypothesis-only market probe. It does not claim market proof, scientific truth, production readiness, or unique capability.

acceptance_criteria: Buyer names one workflow where a proof packet would be reviewed by a real stakeholder.
Buyer identifies a source, action, verification, or replay gap in the current workflow.
Buyer names a concrete artifact that would move the conversation from interest to pilot.
Pilot candidate can ingest traces or agent logs and produce durable action receipts.

outreach_packets.subject: Action-receipt layer for agent tracing and replay
outreach_packets.template_body:
We are testing whether AI infrastructure teams need a proof layer that connects traces, workspace state, tool authority, verification verdicts, and durable receipts.

Pilot ask: Evaluate one agent workflow where existing traces are converted into action receipts with replay and negative-verdict handling.

Demo anchor: Agent observability-to-action-receipt proof packet.

Discovery question: When evaluating a Project Telos proof-packet workflow, how would your team answer this objection: We already have tracing and LLM observability.

Boundary: this is a hypothesis-only market probe. It does not claim market proof, scientific truth, production readiness, or unique capability.

acceptance_criteria: Buyer names one workflow where a proof packet would be reviewed by a real stakeholder.
Buyer identifies a source, action, verification, or replay gap in the current workflow.
Buyer names a concrete artifact that would move the conversation from interest to pilot.
Pilot candidate has an audit or governance review event that needs a compact evidence packet.

outreach_packets.subject: Audit-ready proof packets for high-stakes agent actions
outreach_packets.template_body:
We are testing whether regulated teams need reviewable proof packets that expose source provenance, human oversight, admission records, and verification boundaries without leaking private payloads.

Pilot ask: Score one high-stakes workflow against an audit packet that can show evidence without claiming production readiness.

Demo anchor: Regulated agent action proof packet.

Discovery question: When evaluating a Project Telos proof-packet workflow, how would your team answer this objection: Does this map to AI risk-management needs?

Boundary: this is a hypothesis-only market probe. It does not claim market proof, scientific truth, production readiness, or unique capability.
